Petition Under Probate Code 10350 For Recovery Of Costs And Damages Attributable To Buyers Default On Purchase Of Estate Real Property
Off Calendar Sep-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court noted that Ms. Zhao was not present. Mr. Ferdon stated that there was further damage to the property and that Ms. Zhao realized that purchasing the property was not a prudent deal. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition Under Probate Code 10350 For Recovery Of Costs And Damages Attributable To Buyers Default On Purchase Of Estate Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-31-2010 Continued To Sep-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2025 Keith Street S.F.)
Off Calendar Feb-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The sale was confirmed at $130,000.00. Bond is not required. The Court granted the petition, (subject to the written order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the Petitioner. Pierre Malak (Broker) and Helena Zhao (Buyer) were also present.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the Buyer never closed escrow and that they never filed their own petition. Mr. Malak stated that damage had occurred to the property when in possession of the Administrator, that it was not fair for his Client to pay the purchase price and that a new value should be set as she did not cause the damages. The Court suggested Ms. Zhao hire an Attorney. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2025 Keith St., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Dec-24-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

The sale was confirmed at $142,000.00. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-16-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Clerides for Ricardo Tobie (Petitioner). Robert Dorton (Grandson). Wilbert Freeman (Son). Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

Mr. Dorton requested a continuance. The Court denied the request to continue the matter and appointed the SFPA as the administrator of the estate of Inez Freeman with the authority to sell real property. Mr. Clerides reported to the Court that due to personal reasons his client was unable to act. Mr. Dorton stated that there was property in St.Louis. The Court informed him that he would have to initiate a probate action in that state and to seek counsel in St. Louis. Mr. Ferdon will prepare the order. Mr. Freeman objected to the appointment.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin. Bailiff: T. Bonner. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-30-2007 Continued To Nov-13-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Wilburt Freeman (Son). Robert Doherty (Grandson). Ron Johnson (Grandson).

Mr. Freeman reported that he has spoken to an attorney, but he has not hired one. The grandson's have no intention to obatin counsel. Mr. Ferdon reported that he was informed by Mr. Clerides that his client probably could not act as administrator. The Court indicated that they needed to proceed under the Rules of Probate and continued the matter to November 13, 2007. Mr. Ferdon raised the issue that no final accounting had been filed on the conservatorship and the conservator is deceased. The Court will contact Mr. McCleod to appear at the next hearing.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2007 Continued To Oct-30-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Peter Clerides appeared for Ricardo Tobie (Petitioner). Wilbert Freeman (Son), Robert Edward Dorton (Grandson), Chanelle Dorton (Grandaughter), Annette Youngs (Grandaughter) all appeared in Pro Per. Jonathan Ferdon appeared for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

The parties requested a continuance for the purpose to obtain Counsel. Robert Dorton and Annette Youngs object to the appointment of Ricardo Tobie. Ms. Youngs indicated that she wanted a neutral party appointed. The Court will refer this case to the SFPA. Mr. Ferdon asked for a 2 week continuance and asked the parties to have their attorneys contact him. Matter is continued to October 30, 2007. The Court advised the heirs to return with counsel or be prepared to represent themselves.

Judge: Herbert Donaldson. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth CSR#5162. Bailiff: O. Arias. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-11-2007 Continued To Oct-16-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Peter Clerides appearing for petitioner. Wilbert Freeman (son of the decedent) appearing in Propria Persona.
Mr Freeman requested a continuance so that he could obtain new counsel. The Court continued the matter to 10/16/2007.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-31-2007 Continued To Aug-21-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Steven Wienkauf, appearing for the Public Administrator, Peter Clerides, appearing for the petitioner, Thomas Shelton, appearing for W. Freeman
Mr.Shelton states the Mr. Freeman is entitle to file a petition and ask the court to have time to do so. The court states the matter be continued to 8/21/07 in order to allow Mr. Freeman to file his petition.
JUDGE:JOHN DEARMAN, REPORTER:KATY BESAMIN, CSR #11722, CLERK: LARRISA DOTSON, BALIFF:
